behavior_surfaces: desktop chat store websocket workflow_state handling
desktop queued prompt guide behavior after terminal workflow state
desktop workflow start busy guard recovery for terminal workflow source sessions
desktop session API chat status lookup
state_contracts: Terminal workflow summaries may settle stale busy chat observation state only when there is no active tool, permission, streaming text/input, or pending delta.
Terminal workflow settlement clears stale busy state but does not auto-submit queued outbound user prompts.
Workflow start from a terminal source session may reconcile stale local busy state only after the server chat-status endpoint reports idle.
Active source sessions and active tool execution remain blocking when the server does not confirm idle.

Linked workflow start source-active validation must use the shared chat status state, not conversationService.hasSession, because prewarmed/resumed idle SDK sessions can exist after a workflow has completed.
WebSocket status, message_complete, and permission request messages must update the shared session chat state consumed by the REST chat-status endpoint and linked workflow source guard.

known_unknowns: Local project-cognition database is schema v1 while current runtime expects schema v2; closeout update may be blocked until map maintenance rebuilds or migrates the cognition DB.
The user should manually retry the completed workflow session context picker in the restarted desktop app with a real workflow template.
confidence_notes: First root cause is backed by exact desktop guard source and RED/GREEN ChatInput plus chatStore tests.
Second root cause is backed by live API evidence: the session reported chat-status idle while linked workflow start still returned WORKFLOW_SOURCE_ACTIVE before the server guard fix.
The fix is constrained to terminal workflow summaries, server-confirmed idle reconciliation, and shared chat-state source validation, preserving real active response/tool blocking.
user_decisions: User reported the completed workflow session still showed the wait-for-current-response toast after the first terminal workflow_state fix.
